note: Enhanced PR Freelance scrape — 7-day window. Sources: Mediabistro, ZipRecruiter, LinkedIn Jobs (via Google index), agency career pages, The PR Net. Deduplicated against pr-freelance-scrape reports from 2026-05-04 through 2026-05-19. Skipped repeats: Inkhouse (5/7), The Work Crowd (5/8), FINN Partners (5/14), Crackle PR (5/12), Gregory FCA/BackBay (5/12), Method Communications (5/19), Caliber Corporate Advisers (5/8). All leads have engagementModel indicating freelance/contract suitability. Search clusters covered: freelance PR, fractional comms, agency overflow, white-label PR, contract publicist, earned media consultant.

leads.name: Reputation Management Consultants
signals: Active Mediabistro postings for freelance PR in Denver & Chicago
Specifies real estate, business, financial services experience required
10 hours/week — fits a senior freelancer with multiple engagements
Multiple parallel city postings suggest ongoing overflow need
leads.notes: Strongest immediate-intent lead this week. Explicit 1099 freelance role with financial services + business reputation work. Mark's senior media relations + executive narrative chops map directly. Pitch as 'experienced senior PR freelancer with deep financial services book.' The Chicago posting is a sibling listing — same buyer, two markets.

leads.name: Mission North
signals: Dedicated 'Always Hiring' freelance page (year-round bench)
Active SVP, Fintech Communications role indicates expanding fintech book
Recent Built In NYC posting for 'Freelance Opportunities' generic role
Existing fintech practice = direct match for Mark
leads.notes: Top-tier strategic agency with a year-round freelance bench. Best route is submitting via the Always Hiring page with a financial services / fintech specialization angle. Mention SVP Fintech role as context: their fintech practice is expanding and likely needs overflow execution help.

leads.name: Bospar
leads.compensationText: Retainers $18K–$35K/mo (client side) — contractor rates per engagement
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Contract / Account Associate roles + freelancer bench
signals: 8 active jobs on ZipRecruiter — mixed FT + project
Explicit Fintech & Insuretech practice, plus AI/B2B SaaS
Virtual-first agency uses freelancers for surge capacity
PRWeek-nominated independent — credibility with media
leads.notes: Virtual boutique with a real fintech book. Pitch path: contact Curtis directly via LinkedIn with two sample fintech narratives Mark has placed. Their 80-person staff and $18K–$35K retainers mean they have overflow needs but vet hard.

leads.name: BerlinRosen
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Mix of FT, contract, and short-term project (Jun 1 – Oct 31 noted)
signals: 14 active jobs including time-bounded project role (Jun 1 – Oct 31, 2026)
Recent 4-day-week posting suggests flexible engagement models
Corporate, real estate, and impact practices align with Mark's exec comms strength
Multi-office: NYC, DC, LA — broader media bench
leads.notes: Less financial-pure than other targets (more corporate/policy/impact), but the explicit Jun–Oct project role suggests they're staffing up for a campaign and may need senior media relations overflow. Worth a targeted outreach to the corporate practice.

leads.name: Highwire PR
signals: 21 active jobs on ZipRecruiter — meaningful capacity gap
Strong B2B tech + fintech + cybersecurity portfolio
Series A–IPO client mix overlaps with Mark's narrative work
Distributed model accommodates freelancer integration
leads.notes: Largest active hiring footprint in this list (21 roles). Skewed FT but agencies at this hiring velocity often have side overflow needs. Pitch the cybersecurity + fintech intersection — Mark's voice strongest there.

leads.name: Antenna Group
leads.industry: Climate, Health, Energy & Real Estate Communications
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Mix of FT and freelance creative talent
signals: Active Workable openings + ZipRecruiter listings
Real estate / energy / climate are sectors with growing PE ownership
Existing freelance creative network (writers, designers)
Headcount growth across multiple practices
leads.notes: Lowest-fit lead this week — Antenna's verticals lean climate/health/real estate rather than financial. Including because: (1) real estate has heavy PE/REIT exposure that overlaps Mark's IR-adjacent work; (2) they actively use freelancers. Pitch as senior PR generalist who can plug into real estate practice.
